name: James Weldon Johnson
abstract: James Weldon Johnson (June 17, 1871 – June 26, 1938) was an African American author, politician, diplomat, critic, journalist, poet, anthologist, educator, lawyer, songwriter and early civil rights activist. He was married to civil rights activist Grace Nail Johnson.

about_quotes.speaker: Ellis Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: she introduced our class to poetry: James Weldon Johnson, Richard Wright, Claude McKay.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ellis Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Barry Harris
about_quotes.evidence: There was a writer, black writer named James Weldon Johnson, he wrote the Negro national anthem, Lift Every Voice and Sing. And he, let me tell you this, he wrote a thing called The Creation, and that turned me onto how the music started and how everything does this.
about_quotes.doc_title: Barry Harris — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
